summary: FoodGuard AI is a mobile app that uses the camera and AI to scan food items and packaged food, identifying health risks and providing personalized dietary warnings based on user health profiles.
architectural_notes: Mobile app with camera integration for real-time food scanning and AI-based food identification
OCR required for ingredients label text extraction on packaged food
Health scoring engine: deducts points for high sugar (-20), high fat (-15), preservatives (-10); color-coded result display
imageRequirements:
## Required Images
- FoodGuard AI app logo: a stylized shield icon with a green leaf or checkmark inside, clean and modern on a white background (foodguard-logo.dim_256x256.png)
- Splash screen background: soft gradient from deep green to light mint, abstract healthy food silhouettes subtly blended in (splash-bg.dim_1080x1920.png)
- Home screen hero illustration: a flat-style illustration of a hand holding a smartphone scanning a bowl of colorful fresh food (home-hero.dim_800x600.png)

existing_features.title: Implement a Splash Screen that displays the FoodGuard AI logo, app name, and a brief tagline, shown briefly on app launch before transitioning to the Home Screen.
Implement a Home Screen with a prominent 'Scan Food' button, navigation to the Profile screen, and a brief description of the app's purpose.

Implement a Camera Screen that activates the device camera when 'Scan Food' is pressed, allowing the user to capture an image of a food item or packaged food label/barcode. Provide a 'Capture' button and display a viewfinder overlay.

Implement a packaged food ingredient scanner on the frontend: after capturing an image of an ingredient label, simulate OCR by displaying a text input where the user can paste/type extracted ingredient text. Parse the text to detect harmful ingredients: high sugar, MSG, artificial colors (e.g., Red 40, Yellow 5), and preservatives (e.g., sodium benzoate, BHA, BHT). Display warning badges for each detected harmful ingredient.

Implement a Result Screen that displays the full health analysis after a scan: food name, calories, sugar level, fat level, a visual circular health score gauge (0–100), color-coded status badge (Green = Safe, Yellow = Moderate, Red = Harmful), and a list of any detected harmful ingredients with warning icons.

Implement a Profile Screen where users can enter and save their personal health information: age, weight, and one or more health conditions (options include: Diabetes, High Blood Pressure, High Cholesterol, Heart Disease, Obesity, None). Store profile data in the backend per session.

Implement personalized health warnings on the Result Screen based on the saved user profile: after computing the food's health data, cross-reference with the user's health conditions and display condition-specific warnings (e.g., 'Not recommended for diabetic users' if the food has high sugar and the user has Diabetes; 'High fat content — caution for heart disease' if fat is high and the user has Heart Disease).

Apply a cohesive visual design across all screens: use a clean, health-focused aesthetic with a deep green and white primary palette, bold modern typography, card-based layouts, and smooth page transitions. The design should feel medical-grade yet approachable, with clear iconography and ample whitespace.

new_feature_requests.summary: Camera Food Scan: detect food, show name, calories, sugar/fat levels, health score (0-100), and Safe/Moderate/Harmful status
Packaged Food Scanner: barcode scan + OCR ingredients label scan, detect harmful ingredients (high sugar, MSG, artificial colors, preservatives), show warnings
Personal Health Mode: user enters age, weight, health conditions (Diabetes, BP, etc.), AI gives personalized food warnings
Scoring system: high sugar -20, high fat -15, preservatives -10; 80-100 Safe (Green), 50-79 Moderate (Yellow), below 50 Harmful (Red)
UI screens: Splash, Home (Scan button), Camera, Result (score + details), Profile

Implement a food analysis simulation on the backend: given a food item name (string input from the frontend after image capture), return a structured result including food name, estimated calories, sugar level (Low/Medium/High), fat level (Low/Medium/High), a health score (0–100), and a status (Safe / Moderate / Harmful). Store a predefined database of at least 20 common foods with realistic nutritional data.
